reasoning:
The underlying FRED series, NDR12MCD (National Deposit Rate for 12-month CDs), tracks closely with prevailing interest rates. We know from the data fetch that the rate fell consistently from 1.76 in August 2025 to 1.64 on November 1, 2025. Concurrently, the Federal Reserve cut rates on October 29 by 25 basis points (setting a target range of 4% to 4.25%) and is broadly expected to continue cutting rates through the end of 2025, towards a target of 3.5% to 3.75%. 

Commercial banks typically lower deposit rates in lockstep with or in anticipation of fed funds rate cuts. Because the broader rate environment is strictly downward over this time horizon, 12-month CD rates are highly unlikely to reverse course and increase. Depending on how the resolution interprets the term 'its value on 2025-11-09'—either as the latest published value (which was the October point at 1.68) or the nominal November value (which actually fell to 1.64)—any subsequent readings in December or February will almost certainly be lower as the late-October and potential December FOMC cuts propagate fully into commercial banking rates. The chances of an outright increase are therefore minimal, rising marginally by February due only to a small degree of forecasting horizon uncertainty.
